LEPTON_WORKSPACE_ID.description: Lepton/DGX Cloud workspace where TAO jobs will be submitted.
LEPTON_WORKSPACE_ID.how_to_get: Open the Lepton workspace UI or CLI context and use the workspace id for the target GPU workspace.

LEPTON_AUTH_TOKEN.description: API token used to submit and monitor Lepton jobs.
LEPTON_AUTH_TOKEN.how_to_get: Create or copy a Lepton API token from the Lepton account/workspace settings.

BREV_API_TOKEN.description: Brev API token used to create and manage GPU instances/jobs.
BREV_API_TOKEN.how_to_get: Create or copy a token from the Brev console settings page.

SLURM_USER.description: SSH username for the SLURM login node.
SLURM_USER.how_to_get: Use the username you normally use when running ssh <user>@<slurm-login-host>.

SLURM_HOSTNAME.description: Comma-separated SLURM login hostnames for failover.
SLURM_HOSTNAME.how_to_get: Use the login hosts provided by the cluster admin, for example login-01.example.com,login-02.example.com.

SSH_KEY_PATH.description: Private key path used for public-key SSH to SLURM login nodes.
SSH_KEY_PATH.how_to_get: Use the private key that succeeds with ssh -o BatchMode=yes -i <key> <SLURM_USER>@<host> 'hostname'. If it is not set up, create one with ssh-keygen, install it with ssh-copy-id, trust the host with ssh-keyscan, chmod 600 the private key, then set SSH_KEY_PATH to that private key path.

SSH_AUTH_SOCK.description: SSH agent socket containing a key accepted by the SLURM login node.
SSH_AUTH_SOCK.how_to_get: Run ssh-agent, add the key with ssh-add, and pass the printed SSH_AUTH_SOCK value to the launch environment.

SLURM_BASE_RESULTS_DIR.description: Shared filesystem base directory where SLURM job staging, logs, and results should be written.
SLURM_BASE_RESULTS_DIR.how_to_get: Use a writable Lustre/shared path from the cluster, commonly /lustre/fsw/portfolios/<team>/users/<user>.

SLURM_ACCOUNT.description: Scheduler account charged by #SBATCH --account when the site requires accounting.
SLURM_ACCOUNT.how_to_get: Ask the cluster admin or check a known-good sbatch script for the account value.

SLURM_PARTITION.description: SLURM partition or comma-separated partition list to submit GPU jobs to.
SLURM_PARTITION.how_to_get: Run sinfo on the login node or use the partition from a known-good GPU sbatch script.

SLURM_CONTAINER_MOUNTS.description: Container mount list that exposes shared data/results paths inside Pyxis/Enroot containers.
SLURM_CONTAINER_MOUNTS.how_to_get: Use the cluster's required mounts, usually /lustre for Lustre-backed datasets.

NGC_KEY.description: NVIDIA NGC API key used for nvcr.io image pulls or model/artifact access.
NGC_KEY.how_to_get: Create or copy an API key from the NGC account setup page.

HF_TOKEN.description: Hugging Face read token for gated model repositories required by the selected model.
HF_TOKEN.how_to_get: Accept the model agreement on Hugging Face, then create a read token from Hugging Face settings.

ACCESS_KEY.description: Access key id for S3 or S3-compatible object storage used by dataset/result URIs.
ACCESS_KEY.how_to_get: Use the access key from the object-storage account, cloud secret, or data platform credential.

SECRET_KEY.description: Secret access key for S3 or S3-compatible object storage.
SECRET_KEY.how_to_get: Use the paired secret for ACCESS_KEY from the object-storage account or secret manager.

S3_ENDPOINT_URL.description: Custom S3-compatible endpoint URL for non-AWS object stores such as MinIO.
S3_ENDPOINT_URL.how_to_get: Use the endpoint published by the storage provider; AWS S3 usually does not need this.

S3_BUCKET_NAME.description: Default bucket for workflow outputs when the launcher needs to construct result URIs.
S3_BUCKET_NAME.how_to_get: Use a writable bucket allocated for TAO artifacts.

CLOUD_REGION.description: Cloud/object-storage region used by S3-compatible clients.
CLOUD_REGION.how_to_get: Use the bucket region from the storage provider, for example us-east-1.

DOCKER_HOST.description: Docker daemon endpoint when not using the local default socket.
DOCKER_HOST.how_to_get: Use the value from docker context inspect or the remote Docker daemon configuration.

DOCKER_NETWORK.description: Docker network name to attach TAO containers to.
DOCKER_NETWORK.how_to_get: Run docker network ls and choose the network that can reach required services/storage.

DOCKER_USERNAME.description: Container registry username for image pulls when registry auth is required.
DOCKER_USERNAME.how_to_get: Use the registry account username; for NGC nvcr.io pulls this is usually $oauthtoken.

DOCKER_PULL_POLICY.description: Controls whether local Docker should always pull, never pull, or pull if missing.
DOCKER_PULL_POLICY.how_to_get: Set only when you need behavior different from the local Docker handler default.

DOCKER_AUTO_REMOVE.description: Controls whether local Docker containers are removed automatically after completion.
DOCKER_AUTO_REMOVE.how_to_get: Set according to debugging needs; disable auto-remove when logs/files need postmortem inspection.

HOST_SSH_PATH.description: Host SSH directory to mount into local Docker containers when workflows need SSH access.
HOST_SSH_PATH.how_to_get: Use the host path containing the SSH keys/config needed inside the container, commonly ~/.ssh.

KUBECONFIG.description: Path to the kubeconfig file for the target Kubernetes cluster.
KUBECONFIG.how_to_get: Use the path from your cluster setup command, for example aws eks update-kubeconfig output.

TAO_K8S_NAMESPACE.description: Kubernetes namespace where TAO Jobs, pods, and related resources will be created.
TAO_K8S_NAMESPACE.how_to_get: Use an existing namespace where your account can create Jobs and read pod logs.

TAO_K8S_CONTEXT.description: Kubeconfig context name for the target cluster.
TAO_K8S_CONTEXT.how_to_get: Run kubectl config get-contexts and choose the context for the GPU cluster.

platforms.name: lepton
platforms.description: Managed GPU compute on DGX Cloud through Lepton jobs.

required_credentials.name: LEPTON_WORKSPACE_ID
required_credentials.description: Lepton/DGX Cloud workspace where TAO jobs will be submitted.
required_credentials.how_to_get: Open the Lepton workspace UI or CLI context and use the workspace id for the target GPU workspace.

required_credentials.name: LEPTON_AUTH_TOKEN
required_credentials.description: API token used to submit and monitor Lepton jobs.
required_credentials.how_to_get: Create or copy a Lepton API token from the Lepton account/workspace settings.

optional_credentials.name: ACCESS_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: dataset or result URIs use s3://

optional_credentials.name: SECRET_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: dataset or result URIs use s3://

optional_credentials.name: S3_ENDPOINT_URL
optional_credentials.only_when: using a custom S3-compatible endpoint

optional_credentials.name: S3_BUCKET_NAME
optional_credentials.only_when: workflow needs a default output bucket

optional_credentials.name: CLOUD_REGION
optional_credentials.only_when: S3-compatible storage requires a region

preflight_checks: Confirm LEPTON_WORKSPACE_ID and LEPTON_AUTH_TOKEN are set in the launch environment.
Verify Lepton API access for the workspace before generating runner scripts.
Verify s3:// dataset/result URIs with ACCESS_KEY/SECRET_KEY and aws s3 ls, or provide manual proof for mounted shared storage paths.
Verify model-specific credentials such as HF_TOKEN before generating runner scripts.

platforms.name: brev
platforms.description: Managed GPU instances with Docker support through the Brev CLI.

required_credentials.name: BREV_API_TOKEN
required_credentials.description: Brev API token used to create and manage GPU instances/jobs.
required_credentials.how_to_get: Create or copy a token from the Brev account settings or CLI login flow.

optional_credentials.name: ACCESS_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: dataset or result URIs use s3://

optional_credentials.name: SECRET_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: dataset or result URIs use s3://

optional_credentials.name: S3_ENDPOINT_URL
optional_credentials.only_when: using a custom S3-compatible endpoint

optional_credentials.name: S3_BUCKET_NAME
optional_credentials.only_when: workflow needs a default output bucket

optional_credentials.name: HF_TOKEN
optional_credentials.only_when: selected model requires HuggingFace access

preflight_checks: Confirm BREV_API_TOKEN is set and the Brev CLI/API can list instances before generating runner scripts.
Verify s3:// dataset/result URIs with ACCESS_KEY/SECRET_KEY and aws s3 ls before generating runner scripts.
Verify model-specific credentials such as HF_TOKEN before generating runner scripts.

platforms.name: slurm
platforms.description: Remote SLURM GPU cluster execution over SSH with Pyxis/Enroot containers.

required_credentials.name: SLURM_USER
required_credentials.description: SSH username for the SLURM login node.
required_credentials.how_to_get: Use the username you normally use when running ssh <user>@<slurm-login-host>.

required_credentials.name: SLURM_HOSTNAME
required_credentials.description: Comma-separated SLURM login hostnames for failover.
required_credentials.how_to_get: Use the login hosts provided by the cluster admin, for example login-01.example.com,login-02.example.com.

required_credentials.name: SLURM_PARTITION
required_credentials.description: SLURM partition or comma-separated partition list to submit GPU jobs to.
required_credentials.how_to_get: Run sinfo on the login node or use the GPU partition from a known-good sbatch script.

credential_groups.name: ssh_identity
credential_groups.description: Non-interactive SSH identity for passwordless public-key login to at least one SLURM login host.
credential_groups.how_to_get: Provide SSH_KEY_PATH for a private key accepted by the cluster. Set up passwordless access with ssh-keygen, ssh-copy-id, ssh-keyscan, chmod 600, then verify using ssh -o BatchMode=yes -i <key> <SLURM_USER>@<host> 'hostname'.

optional_credentials.name: SLURM_BASE_RESULTS_DIR
optional_credentials.only_when: user wants a custom results/staging root instead of the platform default

optional_credentials.name: SLURM_ACCOUNT
optional_credentials.only_when: the cluster site requires #SBATCH --account

optional_credentials.name: SLURM_CONTAINER_MOUNTS
optional_credentials.only_when: cluster requires container mounts different from the platform default

optional_credentials.name: NGC_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: private nvcr.io image pulls require NGC auth

optional_credentials.name: HF_TOKEN
optional_credentials.only_when: selected model requires HuggingFace access

preflight_checks: Resolve each host in SLURM_HOSTNAME and require passwordless SSH to at least one host with BatchMode=yes.
If SSH fails, stop before generating runner scripts, ask for SSH_KEY_PATH, and show ssh-keygen/ssh-copy-id/ssh-keyscan/chmod/BatchMode verification steps.
After SSH passes, validate dataset annotation/media paths from the login host using test -e before generating runner scripts.
Verify model-specific credentials such as HF_TOKEN before submitting jobs.

platforms.name: local-docker
platforms.description: Local Docker execution using the host Docker daemon and NVIDIA GPU runtime.

optional_credentials.name: ACCESS_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: dataset or result URIs use s3://

optional_credentials.name: SECRET_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: dataset or result URIs use s3://

optional_credentials.name: S3_ENDPOINT_URL
optional_credentials.only_when: using a custom S3-compatible endpoint

optional_credentials.name: S3_BUCKET_NAME
optional_credentials.only_when: workflow needs a default output bucket

optional_credentials.name: HF_TOKEN
optional_credentials.only_when: selected model requires HuggingFace access

preflight_checks: Confirm Docker is reachable from the launch host and the NVIDIA runtime can see GPUs.
Validate local dataset annotation/media paths with test -e before generating runner scripts.
For s3:// inputs, verify S3 credentials are present and aws s3 ls can read the dataset/result paths.
Verify model-specific credentials such as HF_TOKEN before launching the container.

platforms.name: kubernetes
platforms.description: Kubernetes Jobs on a configured GPU cluster using kubeconfig or in-cluster service account auth.

optional_credentials.name: KUBECONFIG
optional_credentials.only_when: using a non-default kubeconfig path

optional_credentials.name: ACCESS_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: dataset or result URIs use s3://

optional_credentials.name: SECRET_KEY
optional_credentials.only_when: dataset or result URIs use s3://

optional_credentials.name: S3_ENDPOINT_URL
optional_credentials.only_when: using a custom S3-compatible endpoint

optional_credentials.name: S3_BUCKET_NAME
optional_credentials.only_when: workflow needs a default output bucket

optional_credentials.name: CLOUD_REGION
optional_credentials.only_when: S3-compatible storage requires a region

optional_credentials.name: HF_TOKEN
optional_credentials.only_when: selected model requires HuggingFace access

preflight_checks: Confirm kubectl can access the target context and namespace.
Verify job-create permission and allocatable nvidia.com/gpu nodes before generating runner specs.
For s3:// inputs, verify S3 credentials are present and aws s3 ls can read the dataset/result paths.
Verify model-specific credentials such as HF_TOKEN before creating Kubernetes jobs.
